schemaChecks.evidence: All 22 required fields present: slug, title, category, parentTopic, layer, guideType, summary, quickAnswer, whenToUse, preferredAction, backupAction, stepByStepActions, warnings, whatNotToDo, redFlags, preparednessTips, sourceQuality, contentStatus, integrationDecision, upgradesGuide, appTags, sourceReferences. Plus images field included.

whenToUse (4 items), stepByStepActions (0 items), warnings (7 items), whatNotToDo (5 items), redFlags (4 items), preparednessTips (5 items), appTags (9 items) — all flat arrays of strings.
6 source references, each with title, organization, url (or null), and whyUseful fields present.

All field names use camelCase throughout the guide JSON.
slug='solar-still' — lowercase kebab-case, no spaces, no uppercase.

safetyChecks.evidence: All claims in warnings, whatNotToDo, and redFlags traced to research findings: yield figures from Wikipedia/field sources (finding #5/#6), VOC warning from Safe Drinking Water Foundation (finding #9), radiator fluid warning from DesertUSA (finding #11), dehydration/heat stroke red flags from CDC (finding #15), construction energy cost from Wikipedia (finding #5). No unsourced claims found.
No medical or safety claims appear that are not grounded in the research packet. All warnings and red flags traceable to specific sources.
N/A — this is a reference_guide, not an action_card. stepByStepActions correctly empty.
N/A — this is a reference_guide. Warnings are populated regardless (7 items).
N/A — this is a reference_guide. Red flags are populated regardless (4 items).
stepByStepActions=[] — correctly empty for a reference_guide.

Evidence tier is authoritative_plus_field_practice. sourceQuality='mixed' accurately reflects that the guide relies on a mix of authoritative sources (USGS, EPA, WMS) and field-practice sources (FM 21-76, DesertUSA). No purely authoritative source comprehensively covers solar stills — the technique is not in FEMA or EPA standard guidance. 'mixed' is honest and appropriate.

Existing guide warnings preserved: 'A solar still produces very little water and may not cover minimum daily hydration needs' → expanded in warning #1. 'Do not rely solely on a solar still if other safer water sources are available' → preserved as warning #2. Existing red flags preserved: 'Signs of severe dehydration: no urination, extreme thirst, confusion, or dizziness' → preserved in red flag #1. 'Any sign of heat stroke or heat exhaustion while relying on a solar still' → preserved in red flag #2.

duplicateChecks.evidence: integrationDecision='upgrade', upgradesGuide='solar-still' — slug 'solar-still' exists in the guide library (id: 1e37715e-55df-4ec6-98d1-191caf64cff1).
Upgraded guide's category (water_food) and parentTopic (Safe Drinking Water in Emergencies) match the existing solar-still guide.

constraintChecks.evidence: responseRole='supporting' in parentTopic 'Safe Drinking Water in Emergencies', reference layer. The other reference-layer guide (water-purification-reference) has role 'reference'. No primary conflict. Another 'supporting' guide exists (choosing-safe-hydration-sources) but in the scenario layer, not reference layer.
alternativeToGuideSlugs=['purify-water-boiling','purify-water-bleach'] — both are in the same parentTopic 'Safe Drinking Water in Emergencies'.

blockedByConstraints=[] — a solar still does not depend on any specific supply item (no boiling needed, no bleach needed, no power needed). It only requires sunlight and a plastic sheet, neither of which are represented in the constraint tag registry. Empty blocked set is logically consistent.

warnings: One source reference (FM 21-76) has url=null because the manual is a physical publication without a stable public URL. The title and organization are correctly identified for human verification.

alternativeToGuideSlugs links to action-layer guides (purify-water-boiling, purify-water-bleach) from a reference-layer guide. This cross-layer linkage is valid since the solar still serves as background reading for when those action methods are unavailable, but the human reviewer should confirm this cross-layer alternative relationship is intended.
recommendation: Guide passes validation and may proceed to Import/Staging. Two minor warnings for human review: one null URL on a physical publication source reference, and cross-layer alternative slug linkage which is logically sound but worth confirming. No blocking issues.
